worth_it_explanation: Without a CM360 link, DV360 runs without Floodlight conversion data, which means line items cannot count or optimize toward real conversions. The linking setup is a one-time configuration step inside DV360 partner and advertiser settings. Once the link is in place and Floodlight activities are assigned to line items, the platform has the signal it needs to bid toward outcomes rather than proxy metrics.
intro: Display and Video 360 (DV360) relies on Floodlight, the conversion tracking system shared across Google Marketing Platform (GMP), to count and optimize toward conversions. Floodlight lives in Campaign Manager 360 (CM360), and DV360 accesses it through an explicit account link. Without that link, DV360 has no conversion signal and cannot apply automated bidding toward meaningful outcomes. These checks confirm the CM360 link is in place, that Floodlight activities are assigned to line items, and that the attribution configuration is set up so DV360 can act on the conversion data.

audit_checks.title: DV360 advertiser is not linked to Campaign Manager 360
audit_checks.impact_rationale: An unlinked DV360 advertiser has no Floodlight conversion data. Line items cannot count conversions, automated bidding has no outcome signal to optimize toward, and cross-channel attribution across GMP is not possible. The account spends against proxy metrics at best.

educate.base:
Display and Video 360 (DV360) does not have its own conversion pixel. It uses Floodlight, a tag-based conversion tracking system that lives in Campaign Manager 360 (CM360) and is shared across Search Ads 360 and DV360 under a single Floodlight configuration ID. For DV360 to read Floodlight data, the DV360 advertiser must be explicitly linked to the corresponding CM360 advertiser at setup time.

The link is established by connecting a CM360 advertiser to DV360 and selecting the matching Floodlight configuration. Once linked, the Floodlight configuration ID syncs conversion activities, audience lists, and cross-channel attribution into DV360. Without this step, the DV360 advertiser is an island: it can serve ads and report impressions, clicks, and view-through rates, but it cannot record or optimize toward any conversion event.

The link is permanent once created. You cannot unlink a CM360 advertiser from DV360, so the configuration choice at advertiser creation carries forward for the life of the account. An advertiser created without a CM360 link requires a new advertiser to be set up from scratch with the link in place.

A few signs point to a missing or broken link:
  • The DV360 advertiser settings show no Floodlight configuration ID
  • No Floodlight activities appear in the 'Floodlight' section of the advertiser
  • Line items show no available conversion events to assign for counting or bidding
You may want to confirm the Floodlight configuration ID is present in the DV360 advertiser settings before investigating line item or bidding issues. Both depend on this link existing.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For a retail or ecommerce advertiser, Floodlight activities typically include a purchase activity that passes transaction ID and revenue. Without the CM360 link, DV360 cannot count purchases or pass revenue to bidding signals, and return-on-ad-spend (ROAS) bidding strategies have no data to work from.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For a lead-generation advertiser, the Floodlight activity usually fires on a form submission or thank-you page. Without the CM360 link, DV360 cannot count leads or optimize line items toward cost-per-lead targets.

learn_more.source_title: Connect a Campaign Manager 360 advertiser to Display and Video 360
Campaign Manager 360 properties used in Display and Video 360

investigate.intro: Confirm that the DV360 advertiser has a Floodlight configuration ID from a linked CM360 advertiser, and that Floodlight activities are visible inside the DV360 advertiser.

dv360_ui.label: DV360 UI
steps: Open DV360 and navigate to the advertiser you are auditing. Select 'Advertiser settings' from the left nav.
In the 'Basic details' or 'Ad server' section, look for a Floodlight configuration ID. A populated ID confirms the CM360 link exists.
Navigate to 'Floodlight' in the advertiser left nav. If the tab is empty or shows no activities, the Floodlight configuration either has no active activities in CM360 or the link was not established.
If no Floodlight configuration ID appears anywhere in advertiser settings, the DV360 advertiser was created without a CM360 link.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records whether the DV360 advertiser settings show a Floodlight configuration ID, and whether the Floodlight section of the advertiser lists at least one activity. An advertiser with a populated Floodlight configuration ID and at least one visible Floodlight activity passes. An advertiser with no Floodlight configuration ID, or a configuration ID with no activities, is a fail recorded with the advertiser name.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record the Floodlight configuration ID status and the activity count from DV360 advertiser settings above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that the DV360 advertiser for {{site_name}} is not linked to Campaign Manager 360, so no Floodlight conversion data flows into the account. Without this link, line items cannot count conversions and automated bidding has no outcome signal to optimize toward. {{voice_recommend}} creating a new DV360 advertiser with the CM360 link established during setup, selecting the correct Floodlight configuration ID from the corresponding CM360 advertiser. Because the link is permanent once set, the existing unlinked advertiser would need to be replaced rather than modified.

audit_checks.title: Floodlight activities are not assigned to line items for conversion counting
audit_checks.impact_rationale: A CM360 link and Floodlight activities exist at the advertiser level, but without assignment to individual line items, those activities do not count conversions for the line item's reporting or feed its bidding algorithm. The account appears configured but conversions go unmeasured at the line item level where optimization decisions are made.

educate.base:
In Display and Video 360 (DV360), Floodlight activities become available at the advertiser level once the Campaign Manager 360 (CM360) link is established. However, availability at the advertiser level does not mean the activities are counted for any particular line item. Each line item requires its own conversion counting configuration, where you select which Floodlight activities the line item tracks and what counting method and window applies.

When a line item has no Floodlight activities assigned, the line item reports zero conversions regardless of how many Floodlight tags fire on the advertiser's site. The conversion data exists in the Floodlight system but is never attributed back to the line item's impressions and clicks. Automated bidding strategies that require conversion signals, such as maximize conversions or target cost-per-action (CPA), cannot function correctly without this assignment.

A line item that shows conversions in CM360 cross-channel reporting but zero conversions in DV360 line-item reporting is a sign that the Floodlight activity was never assigned at the line item level. The tag is firing, but the line item is not listening for it.

A few signs point to missing Floodlight activity assignment:
  • One or more line items show no conversion events in the 'Conversion counting' section of line item settings
  • Line item reports show impressions and clicks but zero conversions across all campaigns
  • The bidding strategy is set to a conversion-based goal but the line item has no conversion activities to learn from
You may want to check every active line item's conversion counting settings as part of this review. The absence of assignment is not flagged automatically in the DV360 interface.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For an ecommerce advertiser, the purchase Floodlight activity should be assigned to all performance-focused line items and configured to pass revenue so the line item can report on and optimize toward ROAS. A line item with no purchase activity assigned reports no sales and cannot run revenue-based bidding.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For a lead-generation advertiser, the lead or form-submission Floodlight activity should be assigned to line items driving traffic to the conversion page. Without it, cost-per-lead reporting at the line item level is not available and conversion-based bidding has no signal.

investigate.intro: Confirm that active line items have at least one Floodlight activity assigned for conversion counting, and that the assignment matches the conversions the line item is intended to drive.

dv360_ui.label: DV360 UI
steps: Open a campaign and select a line item you want to review. Navigate to the line item's settings and find the 'Conversion counting' section.
Check whether any Floodlight activities appear in the conversion counting section. An empty section means no conversions are assigned to this line item.
For each assigned Floodlight activity, note the activity name, counting method (post-click, post-view, or both), and the conversion window. Confirm these match the advertiser's measurement goals.
Repeat for each active line item across the campaigns in scope. Note any line item whose goal is conversion-based but whose conversion counting section is empty.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records, per active line item, whether at least one Floodlight activity is assigned in the conversion counting section. A line item with one or more assigned activities and a counting method that matches its goal passes. A line item with a conversion-based bidding goal but no assigned Floodlight activity is a fail recorded with the line item name and its campaign.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record the conversion counting status for each active line item above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that one or more active line items in {{site_name}}'s DV360 account have no Floodlight activities assigned for conversion counting. The Floodlight link to Campaign Manager 360 exists, but without activity assignment at the line item level, those line items report zero conversions and any conversion-based bidding strategy operates without a signal. {{voice_recommend}} opening each affected line item's settings, navigating to the conversion counting section, and assigning the appropriate Floodlight activity with a counting method and conversion window that reflects the campaign's measurement goals. After assignment, monitor the line item's conversion column for the first full day to confirm the activity is counting.

audit_checks.title: No attribution model is configured for Floodlight conversion reporting in DV360
audit_checks.impact_rationale: Without an attribution model, DV360 defaults to last-click attribution for Floodlight conversions, which gives all credit to the final click and removes view-through and earlier-touch credit from consideration. Display and video campaigns often influence conversions without receiving the last click, so last-click attribution understates their contribution.

educate.base:
Display and Video 360 (DV360) supports custom attribution models for Floodlight conversions, allowing advertisers to distribute conversion credit across multiple touchpoints rather than assigning all credit to the last click. The model is configured at the advertiser level in DV360 and applies to how conversions are reported and how automated bidding weighs each impression or click in the path.

The default when no model is set is last-click attribution. For search campaigns, last-click is often a reasonable proxy because search captures intent at the moment of conversion. For display and video, the relationship between impression or click and eventual conversion is less direct. A user may see a display ad, later see a video ad, and convert after a branded search. Under last-click, the display and video line items receive no credit for that conversion, so their reported conversion rates are lower than their actual contribution to the path.

DV360 offers several model options including last click, first click, linear, time decay, and data-driven attribution. Data-driven attribution uses the advertiser's own Floodlight data to estimate each touchpoint's contribution algorithmically. It requires sufficient conversion volume to build a model, so smaller accounts may not qualify. Linear or time-decay models are reasonable starting points when data-driven is not available.

A few signs point to attribution being unexamined:
  • The DV360 advertiser settings show no custom attribution model, or the model is set to last click without a documented reason
  • Display and video line items report low conversion volume despite strong impression and click delivery
  • No comparison of attribution models has been run in the advertiser's reporting history
You may want to confirm what model is active before drawing conclusions from conversion data in DV360 reports. The model determines which line items receive credit.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For a retail advertiser running both prospecting display line items and retargeting line items, last-click attribution concentrates credit on retargeting, which is closer to the final conversion, while prospecting display that introduced the customer receives no credit. An attribution model that distributes credit across the path gives a more accurate view of what each line item contributes.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For a lead-generation advertiser, view-through conversions from awareness-stage display or video line items are often excluded entirely under last-click. You may want to confirm the attribution model accounts for view-through windows appropriate to the advertiser's typical path length.

learn_more.source_title: Create an attribution model and change your primary model

investigate.intro: Confirm which attribution model is active for the DV360 advertiser's Floodlight conversions, and whether it reflects a deliberate choice aligned with the campaign mix.

dv360_ui.label: DV360 UI
steps: Open the DV360 advertiser and navigate to 'Advertiser settings'. Find the attribution section and note which model is set as primary.
If the primary model is last click and no secondary model is configured for comparison, note this as an unexamined default.
If a custom model is configured, confirm the model type (linear, time decay, data-driven, or other) and whether the choice is documented in account notes or a media plan.

Review whether data-driven attribution is available for this advertiser by checking whether the option appears in the attribution model selector. Eligibility requires sufficient Floodlight conversion volume.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records the active attribution model for the DV360 advertiser. An advertiser with a deliberately chosen non-default model, or with last-click as a documented intentional choice, passes. An advertiser at the last-click default with no documentation of the decision is a finding recorded with the advertiser name and a note to review the model against the campaign mix.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record the active attribution model from DV360 advertiser settings above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that {{site_name}}'s DV360 advertiser has no custom attribution model configured and defaults to last-click attribution for Floodlight conversions. Display and video line items that contribute earlier in the conversion path receive no credit under last-click, which means their reported conversion contribution is lower than their actual role. {{voice_recommend}} reviewing the available attribution models in DV360 advertiser settings, testing a linear or time-decay model as a comparison, and evaluating data-driven attribution if the account has sufficient Floodlight conversion volume to qualify. Changing the primary model affects how conversions are reported and how automated bidding weights impressions and clicks going forward.
